questions.questionText: A gas turbine starts rotating from rest at $t = 0$ and has angular acceleration $\alpha = 6t\;\text{rad/s}^2$ for 3 s. It then slows down with constant angular deceleration $\alpha = -3\;\text{rad/s}^2$ until it stops. Through what angle does it turn?
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A stone is projected with speed u and angle of projection is $\theta$. Find radius of curvature at $t = 0$.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A particle of mass m is moving with constant velocity v on a smooth horizontal surface. A constant force F starts acting on the particle perpendicular to velocity v. Radius of curvature after force F starts acting is:
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A body is thrown with a velocity of 10 m/s at an angle of 45° to the horizontal. The radius of curvature of its trajectory in $t = \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}$ sec after the body began to move is :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A particle is constrained to move in a circle of radius 30 cm. Its linear speed increases with time as $v = 2t$, where $t$ is in second and $v$ is in m/s. The acceleration of the particle at $t = 3\ \text{s}$ is
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A particle is constrained to move in a circle of radius 1 m. Its angular displacement varies with time as $\theta = 2t^3 + 0.5$, where $\theta$ is in radian and $t$ is in second. The angular velocity and linear acceleration of the particle at time $t=2\ \text{s}$ are given by
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: The kinetic energy K of a particle moving along a circle of radius R depends on the distance covered s as $K = a s^2$ where a is a positive constant. The total force acting on the particle is :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
A particle moves with deceleration along the circle of radius $R$ so that at any moment of time its tangential and normal accelerations are equal in magnitude. At the initial moment $t = 0$ the speed of the particle equals $v_0$, then:

the speed of the particle as a function of the distance covered $s$ will be
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A boy whirls a stone in a horizontal circle 1.8 m above the ground by means of a string with radius 1.2 m. It breaks and stone flies off horizontally, striking the ground 9.1 m (horizontal range) away. The centripetal acceleration during the circular motion was nearly: (use $g = 9.8\,\mathrm{m}/\mathrm{s}^2$)
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: Three identical particles are joined together by a thread as shown in figure. All the three particles are moving on a smooth horizontal plane about point O. If the speed of the outermost particle is $v_0$, then the ratio of tensions in the three sections of the string is : (Assume that the string remains straight)
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: If the radii of circular paths of two particles of same masses are in the ratio of $\mathbf{1}: \mathbf{2}$, then in order to have same centripetal force, their speeds should be in the ratio of $\frac{1}{\sqrt{x}}$ Then find value of $x$.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A coin placed on a rotating turntable just slips if it is placed at a distance of 4 cm from the centre. If the angular velocity of the turntable is doubled, it will just slip at a distance of $\mathbf{x ~ c m}$, then find value of $\mathbf{x}$.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A stone of mass 0.5 kg tied with a string of length 1 metre is moving in a circular path with a speed of $4 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{sec}$. Find the tension acting on the string in newton.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A particle $P$ will be equilibrium inside a hemispherical bowl of radius 0.5 m at a height 0.2 m from the bottom when the bowl is rotated at an angular speed $\frac{10}{\sqrt{x}} \mathrm{rad} / \mathrm{sec}$. Find value of $\mathrm{x} .\left(\mathrm{g}=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{sec}^2\right)$ :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A stone of mass of 16 kg is attached to a string 144 m long and is whirled in a horizontal smooth surface. The maximum tension the string can withstand is $\mathbf{1 6}$ newton. Find the maximum speed of revolution of the stone without breaking it.
questions.subject: Physics
